pattern: A value qualifies only if it is divisible by both two and three, that is by six, so sum and count the multiples of six and floor-divide.
hints: Even and divisible by three together mean divisible by six.
Accumulate the sum and the count of the qualifying values in one pass.
Return the integer floor of sum over count, or zero when nothing qualifies.

edge_inputs.description: given example with two multiples of six
divisible by three but odd does not count
even but not divisible by three does not count
floor rounding of a non-integer average
maximum value that is a multiple of six within range

stress.description: random positive integers within range, many multiples of six
